text: What if chance shapes evolution more than survival? Genetic drift is a powerful mechanism, where random events affect gene frequencies in a population. Imagine a small group of animals. A few individuals just happen to survive a storm. Their genes dominate the next generation. Studies show genetic drift can lead to unique traits in isolated populations. One extreme example? The cheetah. But remember, while it’s a natural process, it’s also unpredictable. Not all changes are beneficial. Curious about evolution? Follow for more science insights!
